name: OUT V3
description: Stereo master output. Two 1/4" TRS balanced line outs · dedicated headphone amp with independent level · CUE input for pre-listening; Main/CUE source switch routes either to the phones bus.

controls.name: Level
controls.description: Dual-gang master level for left and right 1/4" balanced outputs.
controls.detail: 0 to unity · modular ±10V scaled to line level

controls.name: Phones
controls.description: Dual-gang level for 1/4" headphone output. Independent of Main Level.
controls.detail: 0 to full gain · drives low-impedance cans

controls.name: Source
controls.description: Two-position switch selecting what the headphone amp monitors.
controls.detail: MAIN: headphones follow L/R IN · CUE: headphones follow CUE IN only

inputs.name: L IN
inputs.description: Left channel modular-level input. AC coupled. Mono signals here appear on both main outputs when R is unpatched.
inputs.normalizedTo: routed to R channel internally when R IN is unpatched

inputs.name: R IN
inputs.description: Right channel modular-level input. AC coupled. Breaks L-to-R normalization when patched, giving true stereo.

inputs.name: CUE IN
inputs.description: Auxiliary mono monitoring input. Routed to the headphone amp only when Source switch is on CUE. Never reaches the main outs.

outputs.name: L OUT
outputs.description: Left 1/4" TRS balanced output at line level.

outputs.name: R OUT
outputs.description: Right 1/4" TRS balanced output at line level.

outputs.name: Phones
outputs.description: 1/4" stereo TRS headphone output driven by the onboard amplifier. Source selected by the Main/CUE switch.

behaviors.name: Mono to stereo normalization
behaviors.trigger: L IN patched with R IN unpatched
behaviors.description: A cable in L alone sends the signal to both L OUT and R OUT, so a mono bus feeds centred stereo without a mult. Patching R breaks the normalization for true stereo.

behaviors.name: CUE pre-listen without interrupting main mix
behaviors.description: The headphone amp switches to the CUE IN jack while the main 1/4" outs keep playing the L/R signal untouched. Use it to audition a sequence, effect send or new voice in headphones before bringing it up on the main mix.

behaviors.name: Clip indication per input
behaviors.description: LEDs on L IN, R IN and CUE IN light as signal present and redden as the stage approaches clipping. Tune modular gain upstream until peaks stay out of the red for clean balanced output.

behaviors.name: Balanced TRS outputs
behaviors.trigger: connecting TRS cable to L OUT / R OUT
behaviors.description: Both main outputs are impedance-balanced 1/4" TRS. Use TRS cables into balanced mixer/interface inputs for best noise rejection; TS cables still work but drop to single-ended level.

behaviors.name: AC-coupled inputs
behaviors.trigger: patching DC signal to L / R / CUE
behaviors.description: All three inputs are AC coupled, so slow LFOs or DC offsets are blocked and cannot reach the outputs. OUT V3 is strictly an audio interface — do not use it to send CV to an external destination.

behaviors.name: Independent headphone path
behaviors.description: The phones amp sits on its own dual-gang pot downstream of the Source switch. Main Level can go to zero while headphones still monitor, useful for silent practice through the case.

patchIdeas.name: Mono jam on a stereo rig
patchIdeas.patch: Final VCA → L IN only · Source MAIN · Phones up · L IN normalizes to R internally so the same signal reaches both balanced outs and both cans · single-cable master-out for mono patches
patchIdeas.diagram:
OUT V3:
* Source: MAIN

VCA:

- VCA (Out) -> OUT V3 (L IN) // L normalizes to R internally
- OUT V3 (L OUT) -> Output (L)
- OUT V3 (R OUT) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo reverb send
patchIdeas.patch: Dry mix → OUT L and OUT R inputs · stereo reverb returns mixed upstream · Main Level sets live volume to PA · Phones switch on MAIN to monitor the full stereo image in headphones without extra cables
patchIdeas.diagram:
OUT V3:
* Source: MAIN

Mixer:
Reverb:

- Mixer (Out L) -> OUT V3 (L IN)
- Mixer (Out R) -> OUT V3 (R IN)
- OUT V3 (L OUT) -> Output (L)
- OUT V3 (R OUT) -> Output (R)
- OUT V3 (Phones) -> Headphones (In)

patchIdeas.name: Silent cue a new voice
patchIdeas.patch: Main mix → L IN + R IN · Source MAIN during performance · a second voice/sequence → CUE IN · flick Source to CUE to pre-listen the new voice in headphones while the audience still hears the main mix · flip back to MAIN and bring the new voice in via the main mixer
patchIdeas.diagram:
OUT V3:
* Source: CUE (for pre-listen)

Main Mix:
New Voice:

- Main Mix (Out L) -> OUT V3 (L IN)
- Main Mix (Out R) -> OUT V3 (R IN)
- New Voice (Out) -> OUT V3 (CUE IN)
- OUT V3 (L OUT) -> Output (L) // live PA unaffected
- OUT V3 (Phones) -> Headphones (In) // monitors CUE only

patchIdeas.name: Silent practice through the case
patchIdeas.patch: Full patch → L/R IN · Main Level at zero · Phones Level up · Source MAIN · entire modular jams through headphones while the 1/4" outs stay silent · useful at night or in shared spaces
patchIdeas.diagram:
OUT V3:
* Main Level: 0
* Phones Level: up
* Source: MAIN

Patch:

- Patch (Out L) -> OUT V3 (L IN)
- Patch (Out R) -> OUT V3 (R IN)
- OUT V3 (Phones) -> Headphones (In) // main outs silent

patchIdeas.name: Clip-free gain staging
patchIdeas.patch: Working mix → L/R IN · push last VCA or mixer output until the L/R clip LEDs just start flashing red on peaks · back off a hair · guarantees the 1/4" outs hit line-level full-scale without distorting your interface preamp
patchIdeas.diagram:
OUT V3:
* Source: MAIN

Mixer:

- Mixer (Out L) -> OUT V3 (L IN) // watch L clip LED
- Mixer (Out R) -> OUT V3 (R IN) // watch R clip LED
- OUT V3 (L OUT) -> Interface (L)
- OUT V3 (R OUT) -> Interface (R)
